title: Write OTLP-to-internal type conversion functions
description:
The OTLP receiver gets data from OpenTelemetry SDKs but currently discards it. We need functions to convert OTLP protobuf types into our internal rotel-core types.

## What to do

1. Create a new file: `crates/rotel-receiver/src/conversion.rs`
2. Add `pub mod conversion;` to `crates/rotel-receiver/src/lib.rs`
3. Add `rotel-core` as a dependency in `crates/rotel-receiver/Cargo.toml` (path = "../rotel-core")
4. Write three public functions:
   - `pub fn convert_logs(request: ExportLogsServiceRequest) -> Vec<rotel_core::LogRecord>`
   - `pub fn convert_traces(request: ExportTraceServiceRequest) -> Vec<rotel_core::Span>`
   - `pub fn convert_metrics(request: ExportMetricsServiceRequest) -> Vec<rotel_core::Metric>`

## Key details

- OTLP types come from `opentelemetry_proto::tonic::collector::{logs,trace,metrics}::v1`
- Internal types are defined in `crates/rotel-core/src/telemetry/` — read those files to understand the target structs
- OTLP has a nested structure: Request -> ResourceLogs -> ScopeLogs -> LogRecords. You need to flatten this, carrying resource attributes down to each record.
- Trace IDs and span IDs in OTLP are `Vec<u8>` (16 bytes for trace_id, 8 bytes for span_id) — convert to hex strings
- Timestamps in OTLP are nanoseconds since epoch (i64) — keep as-is, our internal types also use nanos
- Handle missing/empty fields gracefully (empty string for missing names, empty vec for missing attributes)

## Tests to write

Add `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in the same file with at minimum:
- `test_convert_empty_logs_request` — empty request returns empty vec
- `test_convert_single_log` — one log record with all fields populated
- `test_convert_multiple_resources` — multiple resource_logs with different resource attributes
- `test_convert_missing_fields` — log record with no body, no severity, no attributes
- Same patterns for traces and metrics

## How to verify

```bash
cargo test -p rotel-receiver -- conversion
cargo clippy -p rotel-receiver -- -D warnings
```

title: Wire storage into gRPC and HTTP server constructors
description:
The gRPC server and HTTP router create signal handlers without passing storage. We need to thread storage through.

## What to do

1. Edit `crates/rotel-receiver/src/grpc/server.rs`:
   - Add `storage: Arc<dyn StorageBackend>` parameter to `GrpcServer::new()`
   - Pass storage when constructing `LogsHandler`, `TracesHandler`, `MetricsHandler`
2. Edit `crates/rotel-receiver/src/http/routes.rs`:
   - Update route handler setup to pass storage to signal handlers
3. Update `crates/rotel-receiver/src/lib.rs` or any public builder/factory that constructs servers
   - The public API should accept `Arc<dyn StorageBackend>`
4. Fix all compilation errors in tests and examples

## Files to modify

- `crates/rotel-receiver/src/grpc/server.rs`
- `crates/rotel-receiver/src/http/routes.rs`
- `crates/rotel-receiver/src/lib.rs` (if it re-exports server builders)
- Any test files under `crates/rotel-receiver/tests/`

## How to verify

```bash
cargo test -p rotel-receiver
cargo clippy -p rotel-receiver -- -D warnings
```

title: Wire receiver startup into CLI run_dashboard command
description:
The `rotel dashboard` command starts the dashboard web server but NOT the OTLP receiver. We need both running in the same process sharing the same storage.

## What to do

1. Edit `crates/rotel-cli/src/main.rs`, function `run_dashboard()` (line ~219)
2. After creating the storage `Arc<dyn StorageBackend>`, also start the gRPC and HTTP receiver:
   ```rust
   // Start receiver in background
   let receiver_storage = storage.clone();
   let grpc_server = GrpcServer::new(receiver_storage.clone());
   let http_receiver = // ... however HTTP receiver is constructed
   
   // Spawn receiver tasks
   let grpc_handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
       grpc_server.start("0.0.0.0:4317").await
   });
   // ... similar for HTTP on port 4318
   
   // Start dashboard server (blocking)
   let server = DashboardServer::new(config, storage);
   server.start().await?;
   ```
3. Add `rotel-receiver` as a dependency of `rotel-cli` in `crates/rotel-cli/Cargo.toml`
4. Log the ports being used so the user knows what's running

## Key considerations

- The dashboard runs on port 3000 (configurable)
- gRPC receiver on port 4317 (OTLP standard)
- HTTP receiver on port 4318 (OTLP standard)
- All three share the same `Arc<dyn StorageBackend>`
- Use `tokio::select!` or `tokio::spawn` to run them concurrently
- If any server fails to bind, log a clear error message

## Files to modify

- `crates/rotel-cli/Cargo.toml` (add rotel-receiver dependency)
- `crates/rotel-cli/src/main.rs` (run_dashboard function)

## How to verify

```bash
cargo build -p rotel-cli
# Then manually test:
# Terminal 1: cargo run -p rotel-cli -- dashboard
# Terminal 2: curl http://localhost:3000/api/health
# Both should work without errors
```

title: Pipeline integration test: OTLP to SQLite roundtrip
description:
Write an integration test that proves the full pipeline works: send OTLP data, verify it appears in storage.

## What to do

1. Create `crates/rotel-receiver/tests/pipeline_integration_test.rs`
2. In the test:
   a. Create a SQLite storage backend (in-memory or temp file)
   b. Initialize the storage
   c. Create signal handlers with the storage
   d. Build an `ExportLogsServiceRequest` with known data (specific body text, severity, attributes)
   e. Call `handler.process(request).await`
   f. Query storage with `storage.query_logs(QueryParams::default()).await`
   g. Assert the returned logs match what was sent (body, severity, attributes, timestamp)
3. Repeat for traces and metrics
4. Test edge cases: empty requests, large batches (100+ records)

## Key imports

```rust
use opentelemetry_proto::tonic::collector::logs::v1::ExportLogsServiceRequest;
use opentelemetry_proto::tonic::logs::v1::LogRecord as OtlpLogRecord;
use opentelemetry_proto::tonic::common::v1::{AnyValue, KeyValue};
use rotel_storage::{SqliteBackend, StorageConfig, StorageBackend, QueryParams};
```

## How to verify

```bash
cargo test -p rotel-receiver --test pipeline_integration_test
```

title: Inject storage backend into receiver signal handlers
description:
The signal handlers in the receiver currently have no storage field and discard data. We need to add storage and call the conversion functions.

## What to do

1. Add `rotel-storage` as a dependency in `crates/rotel-receiver/Cargo.toml` (path = "../rotel-storage")
2. Edit `crates/rotel-receiver/src/signals/logs.rs`:
   - Add field: `storage: Arc<dyn StorageBackend>` to `LogsHandler`
   - Update `LogsHandler::new()` to accept `storage: Arc<dyn StorageBackend>`
   - In `process()`: call `conversion::convert_logs(request)` then `self.storage.insert_logs(records).await?`
   - Remove the TODO comments
3. Do the same for `signals/traces.rs` (TracesHandler) and `signals/metrics.rs` (MetricsHandler)
   - Traces: `convert_traces()` then `self.storage.insert_spans(spans).await?`
   - Metrics: `convert_metrics()` then `self.storage.insert_metrics(metrics).await?`
4. Update all existing tests that construct handlers — they now need a storage parameter
   - You can create a test storage with `SqliteBackend::new(StorageConfig::default())` + `.initialize().await`
   - Or create a mock that implements `StorageBackend` for unit tests

## Files to modify

- `crates/rotel-receiver/src/signals/logs.rs`
- `crates/rotel-receiver/src/signals/traces.rs`
- `crates/rotel-receiver/src/signals/metrics.rs`

## How to verify

```bash
cargo test -p rotel-receiver
cargo clippy -p rotel-receiver -- -D warnings
```

title: Add rotel-dashboard to workspace Cargo.toml
description:
The `rotel-dashboard` crate exists at `crates/rotel-dashboard/` but is NOT listed in the workspace members. This means `cargo test --workspace` skips it.

## What to do

1. Edit the root `Cargo.toml` file
2. Find the `[workspace]` section with the `members` array
3. Add `"crates/rotel-dashboard"` to the members list
4. Run `cargo build --workspace` — if it fails, you may need to add missing workspace dependencies
5. Run `cargo test --workspace` — verify rotel-dashboard tests are now included

## Current workspace members (for reference)

```toml
members = [
    "crates/rotel-core",
    "crates/rotel-receiver",
    "crates/rotel-cli",
    "crates/rotel-storage",
    "crates/rotel-tui",
    "crates/rotel-api",  # This will be removed by archive-rotel-api bead
]
```

## How to verify

```bash
cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | grep 'rotel.dashboard'  # Should appear in output
cargo test -p rotel-dashboard  # Should run dashboard tests
```

title: End-to-end test: OTLP through API query
description:
Write a test that exercises the full path: OTLP data in, API query out.

## What to do

1. Create a new integration test file (e.g., `tests/e2e_test.rs` at the workspace root, or in a suitable crate)
2. In the test:
   a. Create SQLite storage (in-memory)
   b. Start the dashboard server on a random available port
   c. Create signal handlers with the storage
   d. Send OTLP log data through the handler
   e. Query the dashboard API: `GET http://localhost:{port}/api/logs`
   f. Assert the API response contains the log data that was sent
3. Repeat for traces and metrics
4. Test search/filter: send 10 logs with different severities, query with `?severity=ERROR`, verify only error logs returned

## Key imports

You'll need to start the dashboard server in the test. Look at how `DashboardServer::new()` works in `crates/rotel-dashboard/src/server.rs` — it takes a `DashboardConfig` and `Arc<dyn StorageBackend>`.

Use `reqwest` to make HTTP requests to the API.

## How to verify

```bash
cargo test --test e2e_test  # or wherever you placed it
```

title: Align CLI and TUI response models to dashboard API
description:
The CLI and TUI have API response models that don't match what the dashboard API actually returns. Field names differ.

## What to do

### Step 1: Understand the dashboard API response format

Read `crates/rotel-dashboard/src/api/logs.rs` to see the `LogsResponse` and `LogEntryResponse` structs. Note field names, types, and nesting.

Do the same for:
- `crates/rotel-dashboard/src/api/traces.rs` — `TracesResponse`, trace summary fields
- `crates/rotel-dashboard/src/api/metrics.rs` — `MetricsResponse`, metric fields

### Step 2: Update CLI models

Edit `crates/rotel-cli/src/api/models.rs`:
- Rename fields to match dashboard responses (e.g., if CLI has `message` but dashboard returns `body`, rename to `body`)
- Add `#[serde(rename = "...")]` attributes if the JSON field name differs from the Rust field name
- Update pagination wrapper to match dashboard format

Then fix all compilation errors in CLI code that references the old field names. Key files:
- `crates/rotel-cli/src/commands/logs.rs` — display/formatting code
- `crates/rotel-cli/src/commands/traces.rs`
- `crates/rotel-cli/src/commands/metrics.rs`

### Step 3: Update TUI models

Edit `crates/rotel-tui/src/api/models.rs` — same changes as CLI.

Fix references in:
- `crates/rotel-tui/src/ui/logs.rs`
- `crates/rotel-tui/src/ui/traces.rs`
- `crates/rotel-tui/src/ui/metrics.rs`
- `crates/rotel-tui/src/state/logs.rs`
- `crates/rotel-tui/src/state/traces.rs`
- `crates/rotel-tui/src/state/metrics.rs`

### Step 4: Update tests

Fix any test files that reference old field names.

## How to verify

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --workspace
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
```

title: Archive bobkit artifacts to .archive/
description:
Move all bobkit/speckit artifacts to `.archive/` so they're preserved but out of the working tree.

## What to do

1. Create directory: `mkdir -p .archive/bobkit`
2. Move these directories/files into `.archive/bobkit/`:
   - `.bob/commands/` (10 bobkit command files)
   - `.bob/rules/` (6 agent role directories)
   - `.bob/custom_modes.yaml`
   - `.specify/` (templates directory)
   - `specs/` (all 7 spec directories: 001-007)
   - `findings.md` (if it exists in root)
3. After moving, if `.bob/` is now empty, remove it: `rm -rf .bob/`
4. Add to `.gitignore`:
   ```
   # Archived bobkit artifacts
   .archive/
   ```
5. Verify the workspace still builds: `cargo build --workspace`

## Important

- Do NOT delete these files — they have historical value
- Do NOT modify their contents
- The `.bob/` directory may have other files (check with `ls -la .bob/`) — only move the listed items

## How to verify

```bash
ls .archive/bobkit/  # Should contain commands/, rules/, custom_modes.yaml, specs/, etc.
ls .bob/ 2>/dev/null  # Should not exist or be empty
cargo build --workspace  # Should still compile
```

title: Fix stats() to call reader::get_stats()
Test coverage audit and gap filling
description:
Audit test coverage and fill gaps in under-tested areas.

## What to do

### 1. Run coverage report

```bash
cargo install cargo-llvm-cov  # if not installed
cargo llvm-cov --workspace --html
# Open target/llvm-cov/html/index.html to view report
```

### 2. Priority areas to add tests

**rotel-storage** (currently 22 tests — needs more):
- Concurrent read/write (use `tokio::spawn` to write and read simultaneously)
- Large batch inserts (1000+ records)
- Query with all filter combinations (time range + severity + search)
- Purge operations (time-based, count-based)
- Error paths (query on uninitialized DB)

**rotel-dashboard** (currently 4 tests — needs many more):
- Each API endpoint: valid query, empty result, invalid params, pagination
- Health endpoint
- Cache behavior (same query returns cached result)

**rotel-receiver** (67 tests — check for pipeline coverage):
- After pipeline beads are done, verify conversion edge cases are tested

### 3. Test quality

- Tests should assert specific values, not just "doesn't panic"
- Each test should test one thing and have a descriptive name
- Use `#[should_panic]` or `assert!(result.is_err())` for error cases

## How to verify

```bash
cargo test --workspace  # All tests pass
cargo llvm-cov --workspace  # Review coverage percentages
```

title: Clippy clean and warning elimination
description:
Remove unnecessary `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations and fix any underlying issues.

## What to do

1. Find all `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations:
   ```bash
   grep -rn 'allow(dead_code)' crates/ --include='*.rs'
   ```
   There are currently 23 of them.

2. For each one, determine if the code is:
   a. **Actually used** — remove the `#[allow(dead_code)]` (it's unnecessary)
   b. **Will be used soon** (by pipeline work) — keep the annotation but add a comment: `// Used by pipeline bead X`
   c. **Truly dead** — remove the dead code entirely

3. Key files with many annotations:
   - `crates/rotel-tui/src/state/mod.rs` — `DATA_RETENTION_DURATION`, `StateManager`, `ResponseCache`, `PaginatedList`
   - `crates/rotel-tui/src/api/client.rs` — file-level `#![allow(dead_code)]`
   - `crates/rotel-tui/src/state/logs.rs` — file-level `#![allow(dead_code)]`

4. Run clippy and fix all warnings:
   ```bash
   cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
   ```

## How to verify

```bash
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  # Must pass with zero warnings
cargo test --workspace  # Must still pass
```

title: Error handling review and improvement
description:
Review error handling across the codebase to ensure errors are informative and actionable.

## What to do

### 1. Review CLI error output

File: `crates/rotel-cli/src/main.rs` and `crates/rotel-cli/src/commands/*.rs`
- When the API is unreachable, the error message should suggest checking if the dashboard is running
- When invalid arguments are passed, clap already handles this — verify the messages are helpful
- Ensure no panics (no `unwrap()` on user-facing paths)

### 2. Review TUI error handling

Files: `crates/rotel-tui/src/app.rs`, `crates/rotel-tui/src/api/client.rs`
- When the API is unreachable, show "Connecting..." or "API unavailable" in the UI, not crash
- Errors should display in a status bar area
- Verify no `unwrap()` on network calls

### 3. Review API error responses

Files: `crates/rotel-dashboard/src/api/*.rs`
- All error responses should return structured JSON: `{"error": "message", "code": "ERROR_CODE"}`
- 400 errors: include what parameter was wrong and what's expected
- 500 errors: don't leak internal details, but log them

### 4. Review storage errors

File: `crates/rotel-storage/src/error.rs`
- Verify error types have good Display implementations
- Check that database errors include context (what operation failed)

### 5. Search for anti-patterns

```bash
grep -rn 'unwrap()' crates/ --include='*.rs' | grep -v '#\[test\]' | grep -v 'tests/' | grep -v '_test.rs'
grep -rn '\.expect(' crates/ --include='*.rs' | grep -v '#\[test\]' | grep -v 'tests/'
```

Fix any `unwrap()` or `expect()` on user-facing code paths (test code is fine).

## How to verify

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --workspace
# Manual: run `rotel logs list` with no dashboard running — should show helpful error
```

title: Remove 'Made with Bob' comments and committed rotel.db
description:
122 source files contain `// Made with Bob` comments. Also `rotel.db/` is committed to the repo.

## What to do

### Remove comments

Run this command to remove all `// Made with Bob` lines from Rust files:
```bash
find crates/ -name '*.rs' -exec sed -i '' '/\/\/ Made with Bob/d' {} +
```

Also check for any in other locations:
```bash
grep -rn 'Made with Bob' . --include='*.rs' --include='*.toml' --include='*.md'
```

### Handle rotel.db

1. Check if `rotel.db` or `rotel.db/` exists in the repo:
   ```bash
   git ls-files | grep rotel.db
   ```
2. If found, remove from git tracking: `git rm -r --cached rotel.db` (or `rotel.db/`)
3. Add to `.gitignore`:
   ```
   # SQLite database files
   *.db
   rotel.db/
   ```
4. Check the `.gitignore` — it may already have these entries from previous work

## How to verify

```bash
grep -rn 'Made with Bob' crates/  # Should return nothing
git ls-files | grep rotel.db  # Should return nothing
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --workspace
```

title: Rewrite ARCHITECTURE.md to match reality
description:
ARCHITECTURE.md references components that don't exist (rotel-pipeline, rotel-query, RocksDB/sled).

## What to do

1. Read the current `ARCHITECTURE.md` to understand its structure
2. Rewrite it to accurately describe:
   - **Crate structure**: rotel-core, rotel-receiver, rotel-storage, rotel-dashboard, rotel-cli, rotel-tui
   - **Data flow**: OTLP Source → Receiver → Conversion → Storage (SQLite) → Dashboard API → CLI/TUI/Web
   - **Storage**: SQLite with WAL mode and FTS5 (not RocksDB/sled)
   - **Ports**: gRPC 4317, HTTP 4318 (OTLP), Dashboard 3000
   - **Key design decisions**: single binary, embedded storage, async tokio, axum HTTP
3. Remove references to:
   - `crates/rotel-pipeline/` (doesn't exist)
   - `crates/rotel-query/` (doesn't exist)
   - RocksDB or sled
   - Any "plugin system" (not implemented)
4. Keep the document concise — aim for accurate over comprehensive

## How to verify

```bash
grep -i 'rocksdb\|sled\|pipeline\|rotel-query' ARCHITECTURE.md  # Should return nothing
```

title: Code quality review and deduplication
description:
Review code for duplication, consistency, and resource usage.

## What to do

### 1. Find duplicated patterns

Look for similar code across crates:
- Error types: are they consistent? Each crate should use `thiserror` with similar patterns
- Config structs: is there a consistent pattern?
- Async patterns: are all async operations using the same tokio patterns?

### 2. Check resource usage

- Connection pooling: does SQLite use a connection pool or single connection?
- Buffer sizes: are there any hardcoded magic numbers for buffer sizes?
- Memory allocation: check for large allocations (Vec::with_capacity with huge values)

### 3. Review public API surface

- Are types that should be private actually pub?
- Are there pub functions that are only used internally?

### 4. Check for consistency

- All error types should implement `std::error::Error` (via `thiserror`)
- All async functions should use `Result<T, E>` not `Option<T>`
- Logging should use `tracing` consistently (not `println!` or `log` crate)

## How to verify

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --workspace
```

title: Consolidate specifications into docs/system-spec.md
description:
After archiving bobkit specs, create a single accurate reference document.

## What to do

1. Review the archived specs (`.archive/bobkit/specs/001-007/`) for useful content
2. Create `docs/system-spec.md` with:
   - **Crate responsibilities**: one paragraph per crate describing what it does
   - **API reference**: list all dashboard API endpoints with parameters and response format
   - **OTLP endpoints**: gRPC port 4317, HTTP port 4318, supported content types
   - **Storage schema**: SQLite tables, indexes, FTS5 configuration
   - **CLI commands**: all subcommands with options
   - **Configuration**: environment variables, config file format, defaults
3. This should be the single source of truth — accurate and up-to-date

## How to verify

Read the file and verify all information matches the actual code.

title: Add tui subcommand to CLI
description:
Currently the TUI is a separate binary. It should be launchable via `rotel tui`.

## What to do

1. Add `rotel-tui` as a dependency of `rotel-cli` in `crates/rotel-cli/Cargo.toml`:
   ```toml
   rotel-tui = { path = "../rotel-tui" }
   ```
2. In `crates/rotel-cli/src/main.rs`:
   - Add a `Tui` variant to the `Commands` enum (with optional `--api-url` flag)
   - Add a match arm that calls the TUI's entry point function
3. Look at `crates/rotel-tui/src/lib.rs` or `src/main.rs` to find the entry point (likely `App::run()` or similar)
4. The TUI should default to connecting to the same dashboard URL (`http://localhost:3000`)

## How to verify

```bash
cargo build -p rotel-cli
cargo run -p rotel-cli -- tui --help  # Should show TUI options
```

title: TUI usability review and improvements
description:
Review and improve TUI user experience.

## What to do

1. Add loading indicators when fetching data from the API
   - File: `crates/rotel-tui/src/app.rs`
   - When waiting for API response, show a spinner or "Loading..." text

2. Graceful degradation when API is unavailable
   - Instead of crashing or hanging, show "Connecting to API..." or "API unavailable at {url}"
   - Retry connection periodically
   - File: `crates/rotel-tui/src/api/client.rs`

3. Handle small terminal sizes
   - If terminal is too small, show a "Terminal too small" message
   - Check minimum size (e.g., 80x24)

4. Verify keyboard shortcuts are visible
   - The help tab should show all available shortcuts
   - File: `crates/rotel-tui/src/ui/help.rs`

## How to verify

```bash
cargo build -p rotel-tui
# Manual testing: run TUI with and without dashboard running
```

title: CLI usability review and improvements
description:
Review and improve CLI user experience.

## What to do

1. Check help text consistency:
   ```bash
   cargo run -p rotel-cli -- --help
   cargo run -p rotel-cli -- logs --help
   cargo run -p rotel-cli -- traces --help
   cargo run -p rotel-cli -- metrics --help
   cargo run -p rotel-cli -- dashboard --help
   ```
   All should have consistent formatting, useful descriptions, and examples.

2. Add `#[command(about = "...")]` to each subcommand with a one-line description

3. Add examples to `--help` using clap's `#[command(after_help = "EXAMPLES:\n  rotel logs list --severity ERROR\n  ...")]`

4. Check default behavior: `rotel` with no args should either show help or start the dashboard

5. Verify exit codes: 0 for success, 1 for errors, 2 for usage errors

6. Error messages should include next-best-action hints:
   - "Connection refused" → "Is the dashboard running? Start it with: rotel dashboard"
   - "No logs found" → "Try adjusting your time range or filters"

## How to verify

```bash
cargo run -p rotel-cli -- --help  # Review output
cargo run -p rotel-cli -- logs list 2>&1; echo $?  # Check exit code
```

title: Dashboard web UI usability review
description:
Review and improve the web dashboard user experience.

## What to do

1. Check connection status indicator
   - Is there a visual indicator showing if the backend is connected?
   - File: look in `crates/rotel-dashboard/src/static_files.rs` and any HTML/JS assets

2. Verify auto-refresh
   - Does the dashboard poll for new data?
   - Is the refresh interval configurable?

3. Search/filter functionality
   - Test search works on log body, trace name, metric name
   - Test severity filter, time range filter

4. Responsive layout
   - Does the dashboard look reasonable on different screen sizes?

## How to verify

```bash
cargo run -p rotel-cli -- dashboard
# Open http://localhost:3000 in browser and test manually
```

title: Update README with accurate getting-started guide
description:
The README needs updating to reflect the actual state of the project.

## What to do

1. Read the current `README.md`
2. Update the getting-started section:
   - Build: `cargo build --workspace`
   - Run: `rotel dashboard` (starts everything)
   - Send data: example otel-cli or curl command to send OTLP data
   - View data: `rotel logs list`, `rotel tui`, or `http://localhost:3000`
3. Update the architecture section to match reality
4. Remove any references to features that don't exist
5. Keep it concise — detailed docs go in `docs/`

## How to verify

Follow the getting-started steps yourself to verify they work.
